_notes: Source: AU Core Medication (Australian realm). Target: US Core Medication (US realm). Both make code mandatory (min=1, MS). Key difference: AU Core uses AU-specific medication code slicing on code.coding (PBS items and AMT Australian Medication required sub-slices) with no overall code binding; US Core replaces this entirely with a single extensible US NLM medication codes binding at the code level and no sub-slices.

drifts.description: code.coding slicing removed in target: source AU Core defines pattern-type slicing on code.coding with two AU-specific required binding slices (pbs, amt); target US Core has no slicing on code.coding.
code.coding:pbs slice removed in target: source has [mustSupport=true, required(http://terminology.hl7.org.au/ValueSet/pbs-item)] [AU PBS items VS]; target has no equivalent slice.

code.coding:amt slice removed in target: source has [mustSupport=true, required(https://healthterminologies.gov.au/fhir/ValueSet/australian-medication-1)] [AU AMT medications VS]; target has no equivalent slice.

code overall binding added in target: US Core adds extensible(http://cts.nlm.nih.gov/fhir/ValueSet/2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1010.4) [US NLM medication codes VS]; source AU Core has no overall binding on code (only AU-specific required bindings on sub-slices). New US realm binding replaces AU sub-slice required bindings.
